title: setaria adhaerens var. verticillata
intro: Setaria adhaerens var. verticillata, also known as Eastern woollygrass or yellow bristle grass, is a type of grass native to Africa and Asia, recognized by its distinctive yellowish or greenish-yellow bristles and tufted habit. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Setaria adhaerens var. verticillata are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to treat various health conditions, often used to treat coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
